title: Webhooks, Replay Defense, SSRF, Abuse, and Sensitive Business Flows · Runtime Contract: Webhook Ack Async Retry
summary: A webhook parses JSON before verifying raw bytes, accepts replay forever, fetches callback URLs from users, retries non-idempotent work, and rate-limits requests but not expensive business actions. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo webhook ack async retry increment in webhook authenticity, replay, and abuse defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Acknowledge only after durable acceptance, move long work to bounded processing, classify retries, make effects idempotent, and expose operator recovery.
Validate timestamp or nonce window, event ID uniqueness, tenant, type, ordering, duplicate delivery, and durable processing state before effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Webhook Ack Async Retry · Theory Webhook Ack Async Retry
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 9630 in a civic incident API behind one trusted regional proxy. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine raw-message webhook authentication, replay bounds, and SSRF egress policy; then lower the byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at the owning boundary. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
